summary: This notebook performs a comprehensive exploratory data analysis on the games.csv dataset from the NFL Big Data Bowl 2025 competition. It systematically examines game distributions, scoring patterns, and team performance metrics across weeks and seasons using statistical summaries and visualizations. The author highlights home-field advantages, temporal scoring trends, and point differential distributions to characterize competitive balance. Readers should take away actionable insights into NFL scheduling dynamics, team consistency, and week-by-week performance fluctuations that can inform future predictive modeling or strategic analysis.
competition_flow: Loads the games.csv dataset, performs extensive statistical and temporal analysis, and generates visualizations to characterize scoring patterns, team performance, and home-field advantages without building any predictive models.
data_processing: Calculates derived metrics like homeWin, visitorWin, tie, totalScore, pointDifference, and gameType (Close Game/Moderate Win/Blowout)
features_engineering: Aggregated team metrics (average home/away scores, average point differences)
Categorical game classifications (gameType based on point difference thresholds)
insights: Home teams win approximately 53.7% of games, confirming a slight home-field advantage.
Primetime (8 PM) games tend to feature higher scoring for both home and visitor teams compared to morning games.
Team performance varies significantly across weeks, with some teams showing consistent scoring while others fluctuate widely.
Point differentials can be effectively categorized into Close Games, Moderate Wins, and Blowouts to analyze competitive balance.
